 {"id":"git_safety_guard-tlog","title":"Add detailed tracing and logging for heredoc detection","description":"# Tracing and Logging Implementation

## Objective

Add comprehensive tracing using the `tracing` crate to enable debugging and monitoring of heredoc detection.

## Dependencies to Add

```toml
[dependencies]
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }
```

## Log Levels

- **ERROR**: parsing failures, timeout exceeded
- **WARN**: fallback triggered, unknown language
- **INFO**: heredoc detected, command blocked/allowed
- **DEBUG**: pattern matches, timing details
- **TRACE**: full command text, AST details (redacted/truncated as needed)

## Instrumentation Points

### 1. Main Check Flow

```rust
#[tracing::instrument(skip(cmd), fields(cmd_len = cmd.len()))]
pub fn check_command(cmd: &str) -> CheckResult {
    let start = Instant::now();

    // Quick reject
    if global_quick_reject(cmd) {
        tracing::trace!("quick_reject: no relevant keywords");
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // Heredoc detection
    if might_contain_heredoc(cmd) {
        tracing::debug!("heredoc_detected: triggering Tier 2 analysis");
        if let Some(result) = check_heredoc(cmd) {
            tracing::info!(
                blocked = result.blocked,
                reason = ?result.reason,
                elapsed_us = start.elapsed().as_micros(),
                "heredoc_check_complete"
            );
            return result;
        }
    }

    // Pattern matching...
}
```

### 2. Heredoc Analysis

```rust
#[tracing::instrument(skip(cmd))]
fn check_heredoc(cmd: &str) -> Option<CheckResult> {
    let extraction_start = Instant::now();
    let heredoc = extract_heredoc(cmd)?;
    tracing::debug!(
        extraction_us = extraction_start.elapsed().as_micros(),
        content_len = heredoc.content.len(),
        "heredoc_extracted"
    );

    let lang = detect_language(&heredoc);
    tracing::debug!(language = ?lang, "language_detected");

    let parse_start = Instant::now();
    let matches = match_patterns(&heredoc.content, lang);
    tracing::debug!(
        parse_us = parse_start.elapsed().as_micros(),
        match_count = matches.len(),
        "patterns_checked"
    );

    // ...
}
```

### 3. Error Handling

```rust
match parse_with_ast_engine(&content, lang) {
    Ok(ast) => { /* ... */ }
    Err(e) => {
        tracing::warn!(
            error = %e,
            language = ?lang,
            "ast_parse_failed: falling back to allow"
        );
        return None;  // Fail-open (hook)
    }
}
```

## Environment Variable Control

```bash
# Enable heredoc tracing
RUST_LOG=dcg::heredoc=debug dcg check

# Enable timing info only
RUST_LOG=dcg=info dcg check

# Full trace (very verbose)
RUST_LOG=dcg=trace dcg check
```

## JSON Structured Logging

For machine parsing:

```rust
tracing_subscriber::fmt()
    .json()
    .with_env_filter(EnvFilter::from_default_env())
    .init();
```

## Performance impact

- Logging disabled: near-zero overhead (static checks)
- INFO/DEBUG: bounded overhead per event
- TRACE: may include serialization; should be opt-in and redacted

## Acceptance Criteria

- When tracing is disabled, heredoc path performance is not meaningfully impacted.
- When enabled, logs include:
  - tier timings (trigger/extract/parse/match)
  - language chosen (or unknown)
  - rule attribution for matches
  - explicit markers when fail-open occurs (timeout/parse error)
- Logging is safe-by-default:
  - no unbounded command/contents output
  - redaction/truncation available
- Unit tests can capture logs deterministically for at least one positive and one error path.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-tlp","title":"[PACK] messaging.rabbitmq - RabbitMQ Pack","description":"# RabbitMQ Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for RabbitMQ CLI and admin operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `rabbitmqadmin delete queue`
- `rabbitmqadmin delete exchange`
- `rabbitmqadmin purge queue`
- `rabbitmqctl delete_vhost`
- `rabbitmqctl forget_cluster_node`
- `rabbitmqctl reset`
- `rabbitmqctl force_reset`

## Safe Patterns
- `rabbitmqadmin list`
- `rabbitmqadmin show`
- `rabbitmqctl status`
- `rabbitmqctl list_queues`
- `rabbitmqctl cluster_status`
- `rabbitmqctl report`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: messaging.* (git_safety_guard-me0)
- Keywords: rabbitmqadmin, rabbitmqctl
- Note: purge is particularly dangerous (message loss)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queue/exchange deletion blocked
- [ ] Purge operations blocked
- [ ] Cluster operations handled
- [ ] Tests cover common RabbitMQ operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:54.495864565-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:34:59.979987179-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:34:59.979987179-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-tlp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.08521142-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-tlp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.128815852-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-tlp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.168743306-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-tlp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.209623583-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-tlp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.251041181-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-tlp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.294498006-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-tlp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.40095833-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-tlp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.446657351-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ttxv","title":"[PACK] monitoring.splunk - Splunk destructive patterns","description":"# Splunk monitoring pack\n\n## Scope\nCreate a monitoring.splunk pack to block destructive Splunk CLI/API operations.\n\n## Destructive patterns (examples)\n- splunk remove index / splunk clean eventdata\n- splunk delete user / role\n- curl -X DELETE splunk REST endpoints (indexes, saved searches, alerts)\n\n## Safe patterns\n- splunk list / show / search (read-only)\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n- [ ] Pack created with keywords + patterns + tests\n- [ ] E2E cases added via pack harness","notes":"Created new monitoring.splunk pack: src/packs/monitoring/mod.rs + splunk.rs with safe patterns (list/show/search) and destructive patterns (remove index, clean eventdata, delete user/role, REST DELETE /services via curl). Registered pack in src/packs/mod.rs (PACK_ENTRIES, pack_tier). Added unit tests. Note: mod.rs reservation conflict with WildLynx; coordinating.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:00:12.713827888-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:40:06.534341278-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:40:06.534341278-05:00","close_reason":"Splunk pack added; coordination pending for mod.rs reservation conflict"}

This let excluded files slip through. Fix by applying glob filtering to collected files inside scan_paths.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:37:04.227561973-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:37:35.336179002-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:37:35.336179002-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-u5w","title":"Create codecov.yml configuration file","description":"# Task: Create codecov.yml Configuration File

## What to Do

Create a `codecov.yml` file in the repository root to configure Codecov's behavior for PR comments, coverage targets, and file ignores.

## Configuration Content

```yaml
# codecov.yml - Codecov configuration for DCG
# Docs: https://docs.codecov.com/docs/codecov-yaml

codecov:
  require_ci_to_pass: true

coverage:
  precision: 2
  round: down
  range: "60...100"
  
  status:
    project:
      default:
        # Match our CI-enforced threshold
        target: 70%
        threshold: 2%  # Allow 2% fluctuation without failing
        
    patch:
      default:
        # New code should have high coverage
        target: 80%
        threshold: 5%

comment:
  layout: "reach,diff,flags,tree,betaprofiling"
  behavior: default
  require_changes: true
  require_base: false
  require_head: true

ignore:
  - "tests/**"
  - "benches/**"
  - "fuzz/**"
  - "*.md"
  - "scripts/**"

flags:
  unittests:
    paths:
      - src/
    carryforward: true
```

## Configuration Explained

### Coverage Status
- **project target: 70%** - Matches our CI-enforced minimum
- **patch target: 80%** - New code in PRs should be well-tested
- **threshold: 2%** - Small fluctuations don't fail status checks

### Comment Layout
- **reach** - Shows lines covered
- **diff** - Shows coverage diff vs base
- **flags** - Shows coverage by flag (unittests)
- **tree** - Shows file-by-file breakdown
- **betaprofiling** - Shows performance impact (beta feature)

### Ignore Patterns
We exclude test/bench/fuzz directories since:
- Test code doesn't need coverage (it IS the coverage)
- Benchmark code is performance testing, not unit testing
- Fuzz targets are input generators

### Flags
The `unittests` flag lets us track unit test coverage separately from integration tests if we add more test suites later.

## Why These Settings

1. **Targets match CI** - Codecov targets align with our enforced thresholds
2. **PR comments enabled** - Developers see coverage impact immediately
3. **Ignores match llvm-cov** - Same exclusions as our cargo llvm-cov config
4. **Carryforward** - If tests don't run, use previous coverage data

## Files to Create

- `codecov.yml` (in repo root)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] codecov.yml created in repo root
- [ ] Project target set to 70%
- [ ] Patch target set to 80%
- [ ] Ignore patterns match our llvm-cov config
- [ ] PR comment layout configured","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:20:36.95215965-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:46:17.185179732-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:46:17.185179732-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: codecov.yml created, CI workflow updated with Codecov upload step"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-u9kz","title":"Trim ast-grep-language features + make installer box portable","description":"Reduce ast-grep-language build bloat by disabling default tree-sitter feature set and enabling only grammars we actually use; ensure installer box-width drawing works reliably across GNU/BSD sed.","notes":"Working tree already has initial Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock + install.sh changes; verify, fix portability, run quality gates, commit + push.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentGlen","created_at":"2026-01-11T14:06:47.718591637-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T21:31:52.958354568-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T21:31:52.958354568-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: install.sh portability improved (printf vs echo -e, \033 vs \x1b for BSD sed). Binary size reduction from tree-sitter trimming was already in d11670e. All tests pass. Commit: 9f89544"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","title":"[PACK] storage.azure_blob - Azure Blob Storage Pack","description":"# Azure Blob Storage Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Azure Blob Storage operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### az CLI
- `az storage container delete`
- `az storage blob delete`
- `az storage blob delete-batch`
- `az storage account delete`

### azcopy
- `azcopy remove`
- `azcopy sync --delete-destination`

## Safe Patterns
- `az storage container list`
- `az storage blob list`
- `az storage blob show`
- `az storage blob download`
- `azcopy list`
- `azcopy copy` (without delete flags)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: storage.* (git_safety_guard-skt)
- Keywords: az, azcopy
- Note: Both az CLI and azcopy tool

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for container deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for blob deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for azcopy remove
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/storage_azure.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Container deletion blocked
- [ ] Blob deletion blocked
- [ ] azcopy remove blocked
- [ ] List/download operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:39.351997921-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:41:26.801968751-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:41:26.801968751-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented storage.azure_blob pack with 19 safe patterns and 6 destructive patterns. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.753085156-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.795917616-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.840313869-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.884106966-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.927073067-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.97272363-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.832247938-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.878400242-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
